mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS changes versus 20-day forward returns falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive returns do not exceed 55% of instances following rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.5 percent, labor market slack signals weakening consumer demand and corporate earnings pressure, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns remain positive in more than 60 percent of months where UNRATE exceeds 4.5 percent, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in real GDP and retail sales indicates a broad-based contraction in aggregate demand, which compresses corporate profit margins and generates negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint negative signal fails to predict negative 60-day returns with an accuracy above 52 percent, or the interaction term loses explanatory power when added to a baseline regression model.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, a monthly decline in the index of industrial production reflects capacity constraints and slowing output growth, triggering short-term risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during late-cycle phases produce positive 5-day forward returns in over 58 percent of cases, or the predictive power vanishes when the regime filter is removed.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate falls below 4.0 percent, aggressive monetary easing typically signals severe economic distress, which overrides valuation benefits and produces negative equity returns over the next month due to recession fears.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 65 percent of periods where FEDFUNDS drops below 4.0 percent, or the threshold effect is statistically indistinguishable from a linear relationship.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where total nonfarm payrolls decline while the unemployment rate rises simultaneously confirms a deteriorating labor market, forecasting negative corporate earnings revisions and negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined PAYEMS and UNRATE signal yields a Rank IC below 0.01 over rolling 3-year windows, or 60-day forward returns do not significantly underperform the market baseline during divergence episodes.

mechanism_narrative: In a housing downturn regime, housing starts falling below 1.3 million units signals collapsing construction activity and related consumer spending, which directly drags down S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: HOUST readings below 1.3 million fail to predict negative 20-day returns with an accuracy exceeding 54 percent, or the signal loses predictive power when applied outside housing stress periods.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly increases in the index of industrial production reflect strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side momentum, which supports near-term equity valuations and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO changes are followed by negative 5-day forward returns in more than 55 percent of observations, or the signal's out-of-sample Sharpe ratio falls below 0.10 over any 2-year period.
